title: Rasi Tuum
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] of [[Azlyn Rae]]|his [[Rasi Tuum family|littermates]], [[Ahn Rasi Tuum|Ahn]] (killed by the [[One Sith]]) or [[Zho Rasi Tuum|Zho]] (survived)|linebreak=1}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Legacy_25_Rasi_Tuum.jpg]] |name=Rasi Tuum |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]][[Legacy (2006) 25|''Legacy'' (2006) 25]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown {{C|irises}} and yellow {{C|sclerae}}[[Legacy (2006) 26|''Legacy'' (2006) 26]] |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings=*[[Ahn Rasi Tuum|Ahn]] *[[Zho Rasi Tuum|Zho]] |children= |affiliation=[[New Jedi Order]][[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] |masters= |apprentices=[[Azlyn Rae]] {{C|[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]}} }} {{Quote|The weak will always perish.|Rasi Tuum|[[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]]}} '''Rasi Tuum''' was a [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] who, along with his [[Rasi Tuum family|littermates]], [[Ahn Rasi Tuum|Ahn]] and [[Zho Rasi Tuum|Zho]], served the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]] during the [[Sith–Imperial War]] and [[Second Imperial Civil War]]. Eventually ascending to the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]], he [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Azlyn Rae]] as his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. At one point, during a mission to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Roon/Legends|Roon]], he [[Skirmish on Roon|defended]] a camp of [[Miner/Legends|miners]] that the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] [[Rav]] and [[Zareb Syn]] were attacking. During the ensuing skirmish, Tuum [[Cho mok/Legends|maimed]] Rav and [[Death/Legends|slew]] Syn, earning the hatred of Syn's son, [[Jariah Syn|Jariah]]. In [[130 ABY]], at the end of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]–[[Fel Empire|Imperial]] War, the [[One Sith|Sith forces]] of [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] [[Battle of Coruscant (Sith–Imperial War)|assaulted]] the [[New Jedi Temple|Jedi Temple]] on the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. During the attack, Tuum told Rae to flee the Temple and later managed to escape the battle. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Order was scattered, but Tuum eventually made his way to the [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Jedi Temple]] on the planet [[Taivas]]. [[137 ABY|Seven years]] after the [[Massacre at Ossus|fracturing]] of the Order, Tuum was reunited with Azlyn Rae—now an [[Imperial Knight]] serving [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]'s [[Empire-in-exile]]—when she, the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Cade Skywalker]], and their companions arrived at the Hidden Temple. After speaking with Rae, Tuum was attacked by Jariah Syn, one of Skywalker's companions, who tried to avenge the death of his [[Parent/Legends|father]]. The Cathar Jedi Master managed to restrain Syn and explained the actual events of Zareb Syn's death and why he had killed the pirate. Sometime after Rae, Skywalker, Syn, and their allies [[Battle on Had Abbadon|seemingly assassinated]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt, Tuum traveled with a Jedi contingent, led by Jedi Master [[K'Kruhk]], to the planet [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]] in order to meet with [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Fel and his Imperial Knights to [[Mission to Agamar|discuss]] an alliance between the Jedi and Fel's Empire. After K'Kruhk and Fel reached an agreement for a mutually beneficial alliance, Sith–[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Imperials]] [[Operation: Thunderstroke|attacked]] the Jedi and Fel–Imperial delegations, and Tuum participated in the ensuing battle. In its aftermath, both he and Azlyn Rae were unsuccessful in their attempt to rescue [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]] from Sith forces above Agamar. Both Tuum and Rae traveled to the Imperial stronghold of [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]] and informed Fel's Imperial Knights of the Princess's location on Coruscant, allowing the Imperial Knights and the Jedi to rescue her. Krayt was soon revealed to be alive and the war escalated anew. Tuum later participated in the [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|allied attack]] on Coruscant, which saw the final death of Krayt and the defeat of the Sith. ==Biography== ===Early life=== {{Quote|I was on Roon on a mission when I felt a strong disturbance in the Force. Something harsh and deadly. I came upon your father and Rav despoiling the gem miner's camp. The rest you saw.|Rasi Tuum explains the actual events of Zareb Syn's death to Jariah Syn|}} Rasi Tuum was a [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] into a [[Rasi Tuum family|litter]] with [[Ahn Rasi Tuum|Ahn]] and [[Zho Rasi Tuum|Zho]] in the [[Time/Legends|decades]] leading up to the [[Sith–Imperial War]]. Both he and his [[Litter (biology)|littermates]] were found to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitive]] to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and were [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of the [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]], with Rasi eventually becoming a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. During this time, he taught the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Azlyn Rae]] as his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]].[[Legacy (2006) 24|''Legacy'' (2006) 24]] [[File:Duel_on_Roon.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rasi Tuum defends a group of miners from pirates Zareb Syn and Rav.]] At some point, Tuum was sent on a mission to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Roon/Legends|Roon]]. While he was there, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master [[Force sense/Legends|felt]] a [[Disturbance in the Force/Legends|disturbance in the Force]] and came across the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] [[Rav]] and [[Zareb Syn]] [[Skirmish on Roon|raiding]] a [[Mining/Legends|miner's]] camp. He pursued the pirates, who kidnapped a young [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] girl, as they retreated to their [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. After Tuum used his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to [[Cho mok/Legends|cut off]] Rav's right [[Limb/Legends|leg]], Syn grabbed Tuum from behind, causing both himself and the Jedi Master to fall off the ship's loading ramp. The Cathar [[Death/Legends|killed]] Syn and used the Force to [[Force jump/Legends|jump]] on the ship's loading ramp in an attempt to pursue the pirates. However, Rav shot Tuum in the chest, causing him to fall to the surface. The Jedi Master managed to survive both the shot and the fall, and from that point would wonder if the girl he attempted to rescue was all right. In [[130 ABY]], both the Jedi Order and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] were defeated by the [[Fel Empire|Galactic Empire]] and [[One Sith]] forces at the end of the Sith–Imperial War.[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] Both Tuum and Rae were present at the [[New Jedi Temple|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], when the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] forces [[Battle of Coruscant (Sith–Imperial War)|attacked]]. During the battle, Tuum instructed Rae to flee the Temple. Tuum survived and managed to escape himself, eventually making his way to the [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Jedi Temple]], which had been built by Jedi Master [[Rawk|Nat Skywalker]] and the [[Jedi High Council (New Jedi Order)|Jedi Council]] on the planet [[Taivas]]. Two [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the fall of Coruscant, the Sith [[Massacre at Ossus|attacked]] the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], killing many Jedi and scattering those who survived. Krayt then established himself as [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] and began [[Third Jedi Purge|hunting]] the remaining [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. ===Second Imperial Civil War=== ====Rasi Tuum's reunion==== {{Quote|Azlyn Rae?''"
"Rasi Tuum?! ''Master?! I thought you had died on Coruscant! I ran like you told me—then you were gone, in the blasts and smoke! What of your litter mates, the other Rasi Tuums? Are they…?|Rasi Tuum reunites with Azlyn Rae|}} [[File:Warm_reunion.jpg|thumb|right|220px|After reuniting with Rae at the Hidden Jedi Temple, Rasi Tuum asked her whether her loyalty was to the Imperial Knights or the Jedi Order.]] In [[137 ABY]], during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]], Tuum was at the Hidden Jedi Temple when the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Cade Skywalker]] and his crew arrived, having been guided there by his [[Skywalker family/Legends|uncle]], Nat Skywalker, now called "Bantha" Rawk. An [[Empire-in-exile|Imperial]] delegation loyal to the [[Attack on Emperor Fel|deposed]] [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] arrived shortly after Skywalker's [[Mynock (Helot-class)|starship]] landed. While Skywalker, the [[Imperial Knight]]s, and Rawk met with the Jedi Council, Tuum recognized his former Padawan, Azlyn Rae—who was now an Imperial Knight and had arrived at the Temple with Skywalker—and approached her. After a joyous reunion, she asked about the other Rasi Tuums—his littermates—and Tuum told her that while Ahn had been [[Ambush on Daluuj|slain by the Sith]], Zho was alive and searching for any remaining Jedi to bring to the Temple. As they headed to [[Meditation Garden Orenth]], Tuum began questioning whether or not Rae felt loyalty to the Jedi or to the Imperial Knights, who had rescued her after the Massacre of Ossus and completed her training. After Rae explained that she swore an oath of loyalty to Emperor Fel, Tuum opined that her [[Heart/Legends|heart]] would always long to serve the Jedi way. He asked her to [[Meditation/Legends|meditate]] on their conversation as she departed. ====Confronting Jariah Syn==== {{Quote|Think, Jariah Syn. You know Rav—and you know, deep down, what your father was really like. You need to stop wishing your father was better than he was.|Rasi Tuum, to Jariah Syn|}} [[File:Rasi_Tuum_fights_Syn.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rasi Tuum is attacked by Jariah Syn.]] A few moments later, as Tuum began to meditate in the garden, he was attacked by Cade Skywalker's companion [[Jariah Syn]], who sought to kill the Cathar and avenge the death of his [[Parent/Legends|father]], Zareb Syn. Tuum was hit in the back by one of Jariah Syn's [[Yuuzhan Vong]] [[Nang hul|thud bugs]] but managed to evade Syn's [[Blaster/Legends|blasterfire]], disarming the Human by using the Force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|call]] Syn's blaster to him. The Jedi asked about the reasons for Syn's attack as he cut three more bugs in half with his lightsaber. When Syn accused Tuum of murdering his father, the Jedi used the Force to throw and pin Syn against a pillar and [[Plant Surge|tangle]] him with nearby vines. Inquiring as to the identity of his attacker, Tuum allowed Syn, who had witnessed the Jedi kill his father, to tell his version of the events. After listening to Syn, Tuum explained that he sought to protect the miners who were being attacked by Syn's father and Rav, and implored Syn to admit to himself that his father was a bad man. When the Cathar asked about the girl whom Rav had taken, Syn told him that she was not all right, and left Tuum in the meditation garden. Later that night, Tuum—along with Skywalker's crew, Rawk, Azlyn Rae, and the Jedi [[Shado Vao]] and [[Wolf Sazen]]—witnessed as Skywalker's plan to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt was rejected by the Jedi Council. Despite this, Skywalker's crew—Vao, Rae, and the Imperial Knights [[Ganner Krieg]] and [[Antares Draco]]—traveled to the planet [[Had Abbadon]]. After ambushing Krayt, they believed they had [[Battle on Had Abbadon|successfully killed]] him.[[Legacy (2006) 31|''Legacy'' (2006) 31]] ====Ambush on Agamar==== {{Quote|Then it is the will of the Force that we find the Princess and rescue her! ''If'' you can even walk.''"
"''I…can walk…but to ''locate'' the Princess will be almost impossible, Azlyn—let alone rescue her! Better to get to Bastion, tell your Emperor about the Princess so that he knows."''
"''My duty demands that I ''try'', Master."''
"''Determined as ever, my former apprentice. Very well, Master Rae, we will try.|Azlyn Rae and Rasi Tuum|}} Sometime after Shado Vao informed the Council that Krayt had been killed,[[Legacy (2006) 34|''Legacy'' (2006) 34]] Tuum traveled with a Jedi contingent led by Jedi Master [[K'Kruhk]] to the planet [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]], where they planned to rendezvous with Emperor Fel and [[Mission to Agamar|discuss]] an alliance between the Jedi and Fel's Empire. On the night before they met with Fel, Tuum had a discussion with Masters K'Kruhk, [[Hira]], and Shado Vao about Fel and his Empire, with K'Kruhk insisting that the alliance was necessary for the moment. The next day, Tuum, Hira, and Vao were present for the successful negotiations between K'Kruhk and Fel for an alliance between the two organizations. Shortly thereafter, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]], who served in [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Krayt's Empire]], betrayed the organization by arriving and warning the Jedi and Fel's Imperial Knight [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]] that the Sith knew of the meeting and were coming with a [[First Sith Imperial Strikeforce|task force]] to [[Operation: Thunderstroke|capture or eliminate]] the Emperor.[[Legacy (2006) 45|''Legacy'' (2006) 45]] [[File:IKAgamar1-L46.jpg|thumb|right|325px|Rasi Tuum, Azlyn Rae, and Marasiah Fel battle Sith forces on Agamar.]] Tuum, his fellow Jedi, and the Imperial Knights prepared for battle as the Sith revealed themselves. K'Kruhk instructed the Cathar and the other Jedi to defend the Emperor, believing that the galaxy's fate depended on Fel's survival. During the battle, Tuum and Azlyn Rae escorted the Emperor's daughter, Imperial [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Marasiah Fel]], to the Imperial delegation's [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], ''[[Defender One]]''. As the three of them approached the shuttle, a group of [[Predator-class fighter|TIE Predators]] shot at the ship. The ensuing explosion [[Sleep/Legends|incapacitated]] Tuum and Rae, allowing the Sith to capture Princess Fel after the Emperor was evacuated aboard the shuttle without her.[[Legacy (2006) 46|''Legacy'' (2006) 46]] After Tuum and Rae awoke to find that the Princess had been captured, they reached out with the Force and sensed that the Sith were taking her to Coruscant.[[Legacy (2006) 49|''Legacy'' (2006) 49]] Three days after the Sith's attack on Agamar, Tuum and Rae ambushed the Sith [[Yuln]] and [[Bokar]], who were searching for survivors of the battle, and killed them. After finding the deceased Sith's [[Sith–Imperial attack ship|attack ship]], Rae expressed her distress over failing her duty to protect Princess Fel. Tuum insisted that it was the Force's will that she was captured, but he eventually agreed to help Rae with her attempt to save the Princess. They strove to infiltrate the Sith–Imperial [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] above Agamar aboard the Sith attack ship, and tried to board the [[Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer|''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[War Hammer]]''. However, the ''War Hammer''{{'s}} docking codes had been changed, and Tuum and Rae were assaulted by other Sith attack ships. They managed to escape and headed for Bastion to inform Emperor Fel and the Imperial Knights of the Princess's location on Coruscant. After arriving, Tuum and Rae transmitted the proper clearance codes to Fel's forces, which enabled them to bypass the [[Mine/Legends|mines]] in Bastion's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]]. They landed on a docking pad with Imperial Knights Antares Draco and Ganner Krieg, [[Master armorer]] [[Hogrum Chalk]], and the Jedi Shado Vao waiting for them. After disembarking, Draco verbally berated Rae for not successfully protecting Princess Fel. Tuum attempted to calm him down but was Force-pushed across the landing platform by Draco. Vao then warned the Imperial Knight not to strike his ally. Ultimately, Draco, Krieg, and Vao [[Mission to Korriban (Second Imperial Civil War)|successfully rescued]] Princess Fel from the Sith world [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], although Draco was captured during the mission.[[Legacy (2006) 50|''Legacy'' (2006) 50]] ====The war's end==== {{Dialogue|Darth Talon|You want to kill Lord Krayt, our Master.|Cade Skywalker|Oh yeah.|Darth Talon|''That'' I will not allow! ''Kill them!''|Rasi Tuum|That ''I'' will not allow!|attr=Rasi Tuum and Cade Skywalker confront Darth Talon|src=[[Legacy — War 5|''Legacy — War'' 5]]}} [[File:Justdroppingintosayhello.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Rasi Tuum, Rae, and Corde attack the computer center.]] In [[138 ABY]], the Hidden Temple was attacked by the reborn Darth Krayt's Sith forces, and the Jedi had to evacuate to Bastion.[[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]] There, the joint forces of the Jedi Order, Fel's Empire, and the Galactic Alliance planned to [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|strike]] at the heart of Krayt's Empire, Coruscant, and attempt to retake the planet. Tuum joined a [[Cade Skywalker's strike team|strike team]] that was supposed to infiltrate Coruscant before the main invasion force and disable the [[Computer/Legends|computers]] that controlled Coruscant's orbital defenses. The strike team was led by Morrigan Corde—the alter ago of Moff Calixte—and also included Skywalker, Vao, Sazen, Rae, and Masters Krieg and [[Sigel Dare]]. The team was covertly brought to Coruscant on Skywalker's ship, the ''Mynock'', and reached the computer center two minutes before the main attack was set to begin. The strike team's appearance surprised the Imperial technicians and [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] stationed there, and Tuum and the rest of the group engaged them in battle. Krieg, Vao and Skywalker managed to destroy the computers, disabling the orbital defenses and allowing the allied fleet to safely emerge from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] near the planet. As the full-scale battle erupted, Skywalker led the strike team towards the [[Temple of the Sith]] to confront Darth Krayt. At the entrance to the temple, however, they were confronted by a number of Sith, including the high-ranking [[Darth Talon]] and [[Darth Stryfe]]. Intent on stopping Skywalker, Talon led the Sith into an attack on Skywalker's group. During the following battle, Dare was wounded and Sazen was struck down, although he managed to take Stryfe with him. Bested by Skywalker, Talon left the scene in order to warn Krayt of the impending attack. Urged by Vao, Skywalker followed her, while Tuum and the others remained to hold off the Sith. Skywalker was able to kill Krayt, and Krayt's death drove his loyal [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|Sith troopers]] insane, as they attacked the allies and the Sith alike. Tuum and the strike team cut down the Sith troopers, and the battle—and the entire war—was soon over as the remaining Sith fled from Coruscant. In the aftermath of the battle, Tuum was present at the [[Funeral of Roan Fel|funeral]] of Roan Fel, who had fallen to the dark side during the battle and had been killed by Draco. During the funeral, the new galactic government was announced, the [[Galactic Federation Triumvirate]], consisting of the [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]], Master K'Kruhk, and the newly-crowned Empress Marasiah.}} [[File:Rasi_Tuum_kills_Bokar.jpg|thumb|right|300px|While injured, Rasi Tuum kills the Sith Bokar.]] The Cathar Jedi Master Rasi Tuum believed strongly in the will of the Force and felt that every action was guided by it. Although he did not enjoy taking an individual's life, he believed that the Force sometimes willed that a Jedi ended a life, such as that of a wicked person. However, Tuum never killed anyone when there was another choice and sought only to protect those he deemed innocent. Despite questioning whether the Jedi could trust Emperor Fel after the Sith were defeated, he nonetheless helped to protect him when the Sith–Imperials attacked the delegations on Agamar. After reuniting with Azlyn Rae, his former Padawan, and discovering that she was an Imperial Knight, Tuum questioned her as to whether she was loyal to the Jedi or the Imperial Knights. While he understood that the Imperial Knights completed her training, he believed that Rae was truly a Jedi in her heart and that she would always yearn for the Jedi way, despite the fact that her duty to the Imperial Knights was clear in her mind. During the battle of Coruscant, Tuum was determined to protect the other members of the strike team from the attacking Sith led by Talon. Tuum had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] with a short beard and a long mane worn in dreadlocks. His [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were brown with yellow sclerae. By 137 ABY, he had a thin scar running down his nose and was closer in size to the [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] K'Kruhk than he was to Humans. ==Powers and abilities== Rasi Tuum possessed powers with telekinesis, often using this to shove his enemies or disarm them. He could also use the Force to manipulate [[Plant/Legends|plant]] life in order to disable his enemies. Tuum was capable of utilizing the Force to leap large distances. On Roon, he was able to sense a disturbance in the Force and successfully traced the source of the disturbance. He was also able to sense Princess Marasiah Fel's presence after the battle on Agamar. Tuum was proficient at [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]], using his lightsaber to deflect enemy blasterfire, and managed to surprise and kill the Sith Bokar by cleaving him in two. ==Equipment== Tuum wore a set of brown [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]] and a necklace. He used a lightsaber containing a green [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]] up to the time of the Sith's attack on Agamar. During that battle, Tuum utilized a blue crystal in his lightsaber. When fighting during the assault on Coruscant against Darth Krayt's empire, Tuum made use of a lightsaber with a yellow crystal. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Legacy49FormerCover.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Rasi Tuum was originally going to appear on the cover of ''Legacy'' 49.]] Rasi Tuum was created by writer [[John Ostrander]] and artist [[Jan Duursema]] for the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series. He was first featured as an image of three Cathar labeled "Rasi Tuum" in the resource comic [[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]]. An unnamed character who appeared to be Rasi Tuum died in the series' [[Legacy (2006) 2|second issue]];[[Legacy (2006) 2|''Legacy'' (2006) 2]] however, ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 25|twenty-fifth issue]] in [[2008]] revealed that the Cathar who died was Ahn, one of "the other Rasi Tuums," while Master Rasi Tuum still lived. Later in 2008, an entry for "Tuum, Rasi" in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' included a picture from ''Legacy'' 25 and the biographical details of Ahn, with no mention of others named Rasi Tuum.{{CSWECite|III|269|Tuum, Rasi}} This article has treated the discrepancies in ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' as editorial errors due to the publishing process. The cover of ''Star Wars: Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 49|forty-ninth issue]] was originally set to [[:File:Legacy49FormerCover.jpg|feature]] Rasi Tuum and Azlyn Rae.Letters page of [[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]]{{Rebelscum|url=story/front/Dark_Horse_Comics_For_June_2010_130023.asp|text=Dark Horse Comics For June 2010|author="Curto"|date=2010-03-15}} Before publication, it was swapped for a cover depicting Cade Skywalker and Wolf Sazen instead. ==Appearances== *[[Legacy (2006) 24|''Legacy'' (2006) 24]] {{Mo}} *[[Legacy (2006) 25|''Legacy'' (2006) 25]] {{1st}} *[[Legacy (2006) 26|''Legacy'' (2006) 26]] *[[Legacy (2006) 29|''Legacy'' (2006) 29]] {{Imo}} *[[Legacy (2006) 45|''Legacy'' (2006) 45]] *[[Legacy (2006) 46|''Legacy'' (2006) 46]] *[[Legacy (2006) 48|''Legacy'' (2006) 48]] *[[Legacy (2006) 49|''Legacy'' (2006) 49]] *[[Legacy — War 5|''Legacy — War'' 5]] *[[Legacy — War 6|''Legacy — War'' 6]] ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] {{1stm}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Rasi Tuum}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Rasi Tuum |es=Rasi Tuum |pl=Rasi Tuum |deGA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tuum, Rasi}} [[Category:Cathar]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Jedi Masters of the New Jedi Order]]
